mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator simplifies the deployment and management of MongoDB databases on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run MongoDB in a Kubernetes environment without the need for enterprise features.

steps.title: Install MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
To install the MongoDB Community Operator, use Helm to add the repository and install the operator. Ensure you have Helm v3.x installed.

```bash
# Add the MongoDB Helm repository
helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator
helm repo update

# Install the MongoDB Community Operator
helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --version 0.11.0
```

Verify the installation:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default
```
You should see the operator pod running.

steps.title: Create MongoDB Custom Resource
steps.description:
Create a MongoDB resource to deploy a MongoDB instance. Save the following YAML as `mongodb.yaml`:

```yaml
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 4.4.0
  podSpec:
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 100m
        memory: 512Mi
      limits:
        cpu: 1000m
        memory: 2Gi
```

Deploy it using:
```bash
kubectl apply -f mongodb.yaml
```

Verify the MongoDB instance:
```bash
kubectl get mongodb
```

steps.title: Post-install Configuration
steps.description:
After deploying MongoDB, configure RBAC to manage access. Create a role and bind it to a user:

```bash
# Create a role
kubectl create role mongo-admin --verb=* --resource=pods --namespace=default

# Bind the role to a user
kubectl create rolebinding mongo-admin-binding --role=mongo-admin --user=<username> --namespace=default
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator and clean up resources:
```bash
# Uninstall the operator
helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator

# Delete MongoDB resources
kubectl delete mongodb my-mongo
```
Ensure all resources are cleaned up:
```bash
kubectl get all
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator from v0.10 to v0.11:
1. Backup your MongoDB data using the built-in backup features or by exporting data.
2. Upgrade the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --version 0.11.0
```
3. Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n default
```
4. If issues arise, rollback to the previous version:
```bash
helm rollback mongodb-community-operator 0
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator Pod CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the MongoDB Operator pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <operator-pod-name>
```
Common issues include misconfigured permissions or missing CRDs. Ensure all CRDs are installed:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep mongodb
```

troubleshooting.title: MongoDB Instance Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the MongoDB instance is not starting, check the status:
```bash
kubectl get mongodb my-mongo -o yaml
```
Look for events indicating resource issues or misconfigurations. Adjust resources in the `mongodb.yaml` file if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources Error
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter an 'Insufficient CPU' or 'Insufficient Memory' error, check the resource requests and limits in your MongoDB spec. You may need to adjust them based on your cluster's available resources.

versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecated features in this version.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with basic MongoDB deployment capabilities.

resolution.summary: A successful setup includes a running MongoDB Community Operator and a deployed MongoDB instance, with proper RBAC configured for user access. You can verify the deployment by checking the status of the pods and MongoDB resources.
codeSnippets:
# Add the MongoDB Helm repository
helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator
helm repo update

# Install the MongoDB Community Operator
helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --version 0.11.0

apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 4.4.0
  podSpec:
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: 100m
        memory: 512Mi
      limits:
        cpu: 1000m
        memory: 2Gi

# Create a role
kubectl create role mongo-admin --verb=* --resource=pods --namespace=default

# Bind the role to a user
kubectl create rolebinding mongo-admin-binding --role=mongo-admin --user=<username> --namespace=default

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured.
